task_completion.description: Verify the telemetry script is created in the correct directory with executable permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshot showing the script located in '/opt/splunk/etc/apps/search/bin/' and an 'ls -l' command confirming it has executable (+x) permissions.

task_completion.description: Verify the Scripted Input is configured with the correct parameters in Splunk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk GUI (Settings > Data Inputs > Scripts) showing the script configured with Interval '60', Source Type 'os_telemetry', and Index 'system_logs'.

task_completion.description: Verify that live OS telemetry data is successfully ingested into Splunk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk Search interface showing search results for 'index=system_logs'. The events must be single-line key-value pairs containing the exact string 'event_type=live_os_telemetry' and real OS metrics (e.g., memory, disk space).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the saved search (Report) named 'Live_Telemetry_Monitor'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Reports' or 'Searches, Reports, and Alerts' page showing a saved report exactly named 'Live_Telemetry_Monitor'.

task_completion.description: Verify the saved search performs statistical aggregation on the correct index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SPL query for 'Live_Telemetry_Monitor' is visible (either in the search bar or report details) and includes 'index=system_logs' along with a statistical command like 'stats', 'timechart', 'chart', or 'avg()'.

integrity.description: Ensure the script generates real telemetry rather than hardcoded mock data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Script contents (visible in terminal or editor) should use actual system commands like 'df', 'free', or read from '/proc'. It should not just 'echo' static, hardcoded numbers.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Splunk web interface to verify ingestion and create the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk Web UI (http://localhost:8000) being used for searching and reporting, rather than just terminal outputs or text files.

integrity.description: Ensure search results are genuinely parsed by Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Search results in the Splunk UI should show Splunk-generated timestamps, event formatting, and the 'os_telemetry' sourcetype tag, indicating real ingestion rather than a mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The script must be located exactly at '/opt/splunk/etc/apps/search/bin/'. The Splunk Scripted Input must be configured with interval '60', sourcetype 'os_telemetry', and index 'system_logs'. Ingested events must contain the exact string 'event_type=live_os_telemetry'. The saved search (Report) must be named exactly 'Live_Telemetry_Monitor' and its SPL query must perform statistical aggregation (using commands like stats, timechart, etc.) on the 'system_logs' index.
